article_meta.title: Trump strategy document revives Monroe Doctrine, slams EuropeBy Idrees Ali, Matt Spetalnick and James MackenzieDecember

reasoning: The summary largely attributes charged phrases to the strategy document or critics and uses neutral descriptions. Minor interpretive wording appears (e.g., “pressuring Europe,” “reassertion of U.S. dominance”) but overall avoids adopting loaded framing as fact.

custom_reasoning.spin: Mostly neutral verbs; slight interpretive choices like “pressuring Europe” and “stark departure” (attributed) and “reassertion of dominance.”
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Claims are tied to the document or critics; no major assertions made without attribution.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluative elements are generally attributed; a couple of phrases lean interpretive without explicit attribution.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Tone is measured; charged terms appear in quotes from the document or ascribed to critics.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks or character judgments in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intentions or thoughts beyond sourced statements.
custom_reasoning.slant: Covers both the administration’s aims and critics’ concerns with balanced emphasis.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes key perspectives: administration, European officials/commentators, think tanks, critics.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Limited use; “major foreign policy shift” and similar are mild, largely descriptive.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Generally neutral; introduces “unilateralism” in key facts as critics’ concern and repeats “dominance.”
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Does not overemphasize negative aspects; mirrors article’s balance.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Presents government document and expert/European reactions without favoring one.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Avoids adopting the article’s headline framing (“slams Europe”); attributes charged language appropriately.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Represents all major viewpoints present in the piece proportionally.
